rationale: The citation is not only in broad literature lists; the paper explicitly invokes Meyvis et al. (2012) as a mechanism-based account tied to construal level in brand extension evaluation. In Study 4, the authors directly test construal level as an alternative explanation and report it does not explain their effect, while their proposed sequential mediation is supported. They also position their contribution as distinct from recent distant-extension mechanisms that include Meyvis et al. This gives the cited work a clear theoretical comparison role.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Meyvis et al. (2012) as a key prior mechanism account in brand extension research and then develops a distinct mechanism for its own effect. By empirically ruling out construal level in this life-role-transition context, it qualifies when the cited mechanism is relevant.
